summary.intent: Implement task #267: the TreeView agnostic core component (plus DnD reorder variant) for the ars-ui Rust project, following the spec contract and project conventions.
summary.outcome: Full TreeView agnostic core implemented with 110 unit tests, 26 snapshots, spec-conformance, and proptest; ars-collections gained all_keys()/all_nodes() accessors; spec fully synced; PR #695 opened and survived 3 rounds of Codex review (28 total findings fixed); all xci-fast gates green.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() returns only *visible* nodes (iterates visible_indices), not all nodes — ExpandAll and similar bulk operations must use the new all_nodes() accessor to reach collapsed subtrees.

Project-wide convention is Key for node identity (not String); Key does not implement Borrow<str>, so String-based BTreeSet operations (.contains(id), .insert(id)) will not compile against BTreeSet<Key>.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys, but the component must seed selection_state.disabled_keys from TreeItem.disabled flags at init — otherwise disabled nodes remain selectable.
There is a blanket EffectName impl for all Effect types — manual impl is not needed (unlike what the spec implied).
Navigation integration tests live in a single tests/spec_conformance/navigation.rs file, not split per component.

ars_collections::typeahead::State (multi-char buffer, locale collation, process_char_with_locale) is the canonical typeahead implementation used by select/listbox/menu — TreeView should use it, not a hand-rolled single-char matcher.
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — edits must target AGENTS.md directly.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files are hardlinked (same inode), so editing one updates both.
Adapter specs (Leptos/Dioxus) already use Key-based api.branch_attrs(key) references — the Key convention applies project-wide.
DnD primitives (DropPosition, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection, CollectionDndMessages) live in ars_collections::dnd, not ars_components.

DraggableCollection::selection(&self) is a required method that needs live selection state — the bare collection cannot implement it; only the adapter (bundling collection + live selection + DOM hit-testing) can.
Separate open tasks #366 and #509 cover Leptos/Dioxus adapter DnD; the agnostic core owns only the semantic surface (ReorderEvent, drag-state machine, valid-slot enumeration, keyboard DnD protocol).
spec-compile-snippets runs syn::parse_file per block (syntax check only, no type resolution) — blocks only need to parse, not type-check across blocks.
cargo xci --profile fast (xci-fast) is the mandatory pre-push gate covering clippy, tests, snapshots, spec validate, compile-snippets, and proptest.

code.finding: visible_keys() sits in the impl<T: CollectionItem> block. Pure read-only accessors like all_keys()/all_nodes() belong in the no-bound impl<T> block (lines 134-175).
selection::State::select already rejects keys in disabled_keys, but the component must populate that set at init from TreeItem.disabled flags or the guard is silently bypassed.
Canonical multi-char, locale-aware typeahead::State with process_char_with_locale; requires now_ms timestamp parameter — event shape must be TypeaheadSearch(char, u64) not TypeaheadSearch(char).

All DnD collection primitives (DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection, DroppableCollection, CollectionDndMessages) are defined here and already closed/available from issue #549.

New file. Key patterns: effective_selection_mode() for TreeItem-derived vs prop-declared mode; clamp_focus_to_visible() after collapse/item removal; apply_selection()/normalize_selection() as the single path for controlled selection; is_interactive_branch() guards expansion and ArrowRight navigation; has_loaded_children() gates ArrowRight entry into lazy branches.

Canonical pattern for callback+effect wiring: store on_checked_change: Option<Callback<...>> in Props, emit Effect::CheckedChange in transition, execute callback in effect setup closure via PendingEffect::run(ctx, props, send).

workflow: TDD is enforced: write failing tests first, confirm red, implement, confirm green — deviating from this order surfaces behavioral drift during audit phases.

The post-implementation audit runs 3 phases: (1) spec/impl drift walk, (2) multi-round adjacent-surface checks (on_props_changed convention, undocumented methods), (3) coverage + mutation. Each phase can surface real bugs not caught by initial tests.
Mandatory pre-PR mutation gate for agnostic components was removed — Nightly CI now owns mutant triage; surviving mutants are addressed when CI surfaces them.
Codex review loop: post @codex review, poll for 👍 or findings, address each finding with TDD (regression test first), reply inline to thread, resolve, re-trigger; repeat until 👍.
Snapshot tests with insta: first run generates .new files; accept with INSTA_UPDATE=new or cargo insta accept; must run --unreferenced=reject to catch orphaned snapshots.
When staging files for commit, avoid git add -A — use explicit file paths to exclude runtime artifacts (.lock files, mutants.out/) not covered by .gitignore.
Pre-commit hooks can re-stage all tracked modifications, collapsing an intended multi-commit split into a single commit — verify staging state after hook runs before assuming the split held.

Background tasks scheduled with ScheduleWakeup/run_in_background fire stale wakeups after being cancelled — these are harmless but require verifying the task-notification output to confirm it is not the active run.

friction: TypeaheadSearch event shape change from (char) to (char, u64) required updating ~8 test sites and the proptest arbitrary — a ripple that would have been avoided by using the canonical typeahead::State from the start.
Three rounds of Codex review (28 total findings) before approval — each round required TDD fixes, spec sync, xci-fast, commit/push, inline replies, thread resolution, and re-trigger.
Background mutation runner contended on cargo lock with other concurrent cargo processes, causing the progress poller to exit with code 1 (not a real failure, but confusing).
Stale fallback ScheduleWakeup notifications fired after the mutation gate was cancelled, requiring context reconstruction to confirm they were not the active run.
CLAUDE.md being a symlink to AGENTS.md was not surfaced until an edit was attempted — required a readlink step to find the real target.

The spec's String-based identity throughout §1–§6 (event payloads, Api params, variant sections) required a broad find-and-replace pass; the mismatch with Key was a silent compile-time failure waiting to happen.
cargo xci-fast with mutation testing takes ~36 minutes — long feedback loop for the final gate.
Pre-commit hook collapsed an intended two-commit split (feature + policy) into one commit, requiring an amend to fix the message.
open_items: Adapter-layer DnD (DOM pointer/keyboard hit-testing, 500ms hover-expand timer, use_drag/use_drop wiring, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls bundling live selection) deferred to #366 (TreeView Leptos adapter) and #509 (TreeView Dioxus adapter).
Lazy Loading (§5) and Renamable Nodes (§6) variants remain out-of-scope no_check blocks in the spec — not implemented in the agnostic core.
Mutation testing for tree_view deferred to Nightly CI — the rerun after adding killer tests was stopped before confirming 0 MISSED; Nightly CI will surface any survivors.
aria-multiselectable now reflects effective_selection_mode — adapter snapshots may need regeneration when the Leptos/Dioxus adapters are implemented in #366/#509.

The agnostic-component mutation-gate policy change (AGENTS.md + post-implementation-audit skill) should be reviewed against other component types (adapters, utility) to confirm the scope is correctly bounded to agnostic cores only.
